Did you ever hear the tragedy of A_Seagull ? I thought not. It’s not a story the people would tell you. It’s a legend. A_Seagull was a streamer so powerful that he literally could save the world by keeping his people entertained. He became so powerful… the only thing he was afraid of were platformers. He could save others from being bored, but not himself from falling down.

Each platform he conquers my eyes light up. Every jump he makes makes my heart soar. When will he make it. they say? Will he ever gain victory, they say? All for the promised glory of shitty loot. But at the end of it all, I can say with full satisfaction, "I was there."
In a few weeks, chat will be asking "Were you there when Seagull made it to the end of the platforms". With a grimace and a tear, I will slowly nod and say ,"Aye, I was there."
¦---¦Last Part Ladder ¦---¦

Seagull jumps, and now my watch begins. It shall not end until my death. I shall watch no other streams, hold no other subs, father no follows. I shall live and die at this stream. I am the spammer in the darkness. I am the watcher behind the screen. I am the spark that stokes the hope of success. I pledge my life and honor to this platforming stream, for this night and all the nights to come.

The year is 2020. My family was celebrating Christmas without me. My friends went out watching fireworks at new year's eve. In the meanwhile I have been here. I almost died to hunger and thirst. But for each platform he conquers my eyes light up. Every jump he makes makes my heart soar. But at the end of it all, I can say with full satisfaction, "I was here Pog".

The year is 2069. My family found my body in the basement. My friends mourned at my funeral. In the meanwhile my spirit has been here. I died to hunger and thirst. But for each platform he conquers my eyes light up. Every jump he makes makes my heart soar. But at the end of it all, I can say with full satisfaction, "I was here Pog ".

The year is 2085. Seagull lies on his deathbed, the countless tries to beat the platforming level finally having taken their toll. He struggles to adjust his eyes to the glare of his computer screen, yearning to view his beloved twitch chat one more time. All he sees is degenerate copypasta. His eyes brim with tears. He grabs the mouse & keyboard, and with his newfound strength, he boots up "Darksiders Genesis" for one last run. He beats it first try.

The year is 3019. My family is dead. My friends are dead. In the meanwhile I have been dead. I died to hunger and thirst. But for each platform he conquers I come alive a little. Every jump he makes makes my soul return. Despite always being on the cusp of life and death, I can say with full satisfaction, “I was here.”

The year is... what even is time? Where am I? What happened to all my friends, all my loved ones? Have I done anything of worth? He misses another jump. Curses. Makes one. Another mark on the wall. There shall be no solace, no rest until we are finished.

Why are we still here? Just to suffer? Every try, I can see him getting lost… and restarting… even seeking new paths. The pillars he missed… the watchers he lost… won’t stop hurting… It’s like they’re all still there. You feel it, too, don’t you?

Loss streaks never feel good. Before each match, we make a prediction about which team will win the game, based on the information we have about the players on both teams. Since most matches will have a team that has a slightly higher chance of winning, placing a player on the team with the higher chance who is currently on a losing streak is aimed at helping them have a fair chance of breaking that streak. While this won’t guarantee they’ll win, it does provide a helping advantage.
